summary: Implemented post-workout feeling page with fatigue/satisfaction sliders, per-exercise notes, and overall note input. Created FeelingScreen component, feeling-helpers module with pure functions for volume calculation, training summary formatting, warning detection, and exercise filtering.
keyDecisions: Extracted all business logic into feeling-helpers.ts for testability (pure functions: computeTotalVolume, formatTrainingSummary, shouldShowWarning, getCompletedExercises, formatExerciseSummary)
FeelingScreen is presentational - receives state and callbacks from parent page
Default fatigue=6, satisfaction=7 per AC (UI design doc says default 5 but task AC specifies 6/7)
Warning shown when fatigue >= 8 AND satisfaction <= 4 with orange styling and '建议下次降低强度' message
Per-exercise note cards only show completed exercises (skipped/excluded filtered out)
Volume computed from all completed sets across all exercises (actual_weight * actual_reps)
Compact exercise summary format: '卧推 60kg×5×3' for same weight/reps, detailed for mixed

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Page auto-enters after workout completion (no back button to workout page)
Training summary at top: training type + total volume
Fatigue slider (1-10) with gradient label, default=6
Satisfaction slider (1-10) with gradient label, default=7
Per-exercise note cards: shows exercise name + weight×sets×reps + text input
Save button saves and returns to calendar
Skip button skips with defaults and returns to calendar
High fatigue (≥8) + low satisfaction (≤4) → orange warning labels
Save triggers ProgressiveOverload.recordResult for all exercises

notes: ProgressiveOverload.recordResult is already called per-exercise in workout.store.recordSet during workout execution. The FeelingScreen onSave callback provides the interface for the parent page to trigger additional overload updates if needed. The FeelingScreen itself is a presentational component that emits save/skip events to the parent orchestrator.
